event: admitted with body mass index of 67 kg/m2 and multiple comorbidities
back pain
gastroesophageal reflux disease
obstructive sleep apnea
hyperlipidemia
hypertension
underwent a Da Vinci-assisted, laparoscopic, one-stage, single-anastomosis DS procedure
sleeve gastrectomy
duodenum was transected 3 cm from the pylorus
duodenal stump was oversewn with a running absorbable suture
two-layer hand sewn anastomosis between the duodenum and the ileum was created
anastomosis was tested with methylene blue and air
drain was placed in left upper quadrant
stomach was removed
upper gastrointestinal study (UGI) on postoperative day (POD) 2 was negative for a leak
Bariatric phase 1 diet was started
discharged on POD 3
developed persistent nausea, fatigue, and severe abdominal pain on POD 11
tachycardic
anemic
leukocytosis
abdominal exam revealed significant distension with guarding
UGI was negative
computed tomography (CT) of the abdomen showing free fluid suggestive of a leak or bleeding
taken to the operating room (OR) for diagnostic laparoscopy
found to have hemoperitoneum
hemoperitoneum was evacuated
active bleeding source was not identifiable
complete inspection of the gastroileostomy anastomosis was performed without significant findings
no leakage seen with a methylene blue test
attempt to explore the duodenal stump was made but was unsuccessful
three large 19F Blake drains were placed
transfused with 2 units of packed red blood cells (PRBCs) intra-operatively
kept intubated and sent to intensive care unit (ICU) for further monitoring
doing well, and transferred to step down on POD 3 after the first OR
became hypotensive, unresponsive to fluids
abdominal drain had increased sanguinous output
second drain showed frank bile
elevated bilirubin and amylase
became tachycardic
taken back to the OR for exploratory laparotomy
underwent laparotomy through a generous right subcostal incision
large amount of hemoperitoneum was encountered
hemoperitoneum was temporized with packing
identified as coming from the gastroduodenal artery which was over-sewn
duodenal stump was found to have total stump blow
staple line was completely undone
Malecot tube was placed in the duodenum and over-sewn with a purse-string suture
abdomen was closed and drains were placed
blood loss was 2500 cc
received 5 units of PRBCs and 4 units of fresh frozen plasma intra-operatively
transferred to the ICU
post-operative course was complicated by hemorrhagic fluid collections seen on CT scan
atelectasis
developed acute thrombosis of the left internal jugular and subclavian veins at a previous central catheter site
without pulmonary embolism
placed on therapeutic Lovenox and octreotide
given intravenous piperacillin/taxobactam
intravenous piperacillin/taxobactam was later changed to cefazolin
successfully discharged on POD 17
